basic_details.job_title: ESIC Medical College & Hospital Chennai Senior Resident Recruitment 2026 – Walk in for 60 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: ESIC Medical College & Hospital, K.K. Nagar, Chennai – 600 078
basic_details.short_information: ESIC Medical College & Hospital, K.K. Nagar, Chennai has released an official advertisement for the engagement of 60 Senior Resident (3-Year Tenure) posts under the 7th Central Pay Commission (7th CPC) on a contractual basis. The recruitment is being conducted through a walk-in interview on July 8 and 9, 2026, across 23 medical departments. This is a significant opportunity for MD/MS/DNB/DM/MCh-qualified doctors seeking a structured, well-paid government hospital residency in Tamil Nadu's capital. Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria as per NMC-TEQ 2025 read with PGMER 2023 can appear

application_fee.details: The ESIC Senior Resident 2026 walk-in interview requires payment of a non-refundable application fee for most categories. Here is a clear breakdown. | Mode of Payment: Demand Draft only, drawn in favour of "ESI Fund Account No. 1", payable at Chennai, on any scheduled bank. The DD must be issued after the date of this advertisement (i.e., after 17.06.2026). Cash, UPI, or online transfer are not accepted.

qualification.education_qualification: MD / MS / DNB / DM / MCh / M.Sc.+Ph.D.
qualification.details: Understanding the eligibility requirements is critical before appearing for the ESIC Senior Resident walk-in interview 2026. Below are the academic qualifications, age limits, and departmental preferences as per NMC-TEQ 2025 and PGMER 2023 norms. | Clinical specialties (broad specialties): Upper age limit - 45 years Pre-clinical / Para-clinical subjects (Anatomy, Biochemistry, Physiology, Pharmacology, Microbiology, Pathology, Forensic Medicine, Community Medicine): Upper age limit - 50 years | A candidate must hold one of the following: | MD / MS / DNB (Postgraduate degree in Doctor of Medicine, Master of Surgery, or Diplomate of National Board), or M.Sc. with Ph.D. in medical subjects - Anatomy, Biochemistry, Physiology, Pharmacology, or Microbiology | All candidates must satisfy eligibility as per NMC Teachers Eligibility Qualifications (TEQ) 2025 read with MSR/PGMER 2023 as on the date of interview. | This is a critical section for candidates - check your department's preferred order before appearing at the ESIC Chennai walk-in interview. | Two departments allow candidates from related specialties to also apply - this significantly widens eligibility for those departments: | Emergency Medicine: Candidates holding MD/MS/DNB in General Medicine, Anaesthesia, Respiratory Medicine, General Surgery, or Orthopaedics can appear - in addition to their own specialty. Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation: Candidates with MD (General Medicine) + Diploma in PMR, or MS/DNB in General Surgery or Orthopaedics are also eligible.

salary_details.details: One of the most sought-after aspects of ESIC Senior Resident recruitment is its competitive pay structure under the 7th Pay Commission. Here is a full breakdown. | Pay Level: Level-11 under 7th CPC Basic Pay: ₹67,700/- per month Gross Emoluments (as of January 2026): Approximately ₹1,60,045/- per month (inclusive of DA and other applicable allowances) DA Revision: Remuneration will be revised in line with Central Government DA revision notifications from ESIC Headquarters. Private Practice: Strictly not permitted during the tenure. Tenure: 3 years (subject to satisfactory annual performance evaluation). No extension beyond 3 years.

selection_process: The ESIC Chennai Senior Resident 2026 selection is primarily interview-based
but there is an important provision for screening if footfall is high. Here is exactly how the process works.

Document Verification (9:00 AM – 11:00 AM): All candidates must report during this window. Only those whose documents pass verification will proceed to the interview. Screening Test (if applicable): If the candidate-to-post ratio exceeds 1:10

a screening test may be held to shortlist candidates at a 1:5 ratio. The test will consist of MCQs in the concerned discipline. Screening test marks do not carry weight in the final result. Walk-In Interview: Final selection is based solely on interview performance before the Selection Board. Result Publication: Results will be posted on the official website www.esic.gov.in/recruitments. Joining: Selected candidates must join immediately upon receiving the offer of engagement.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies in ESIC Chennai Senior Resident Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: ESIC Medical College & Hospital, K.K. Nagar, Chennai has announced 60 Senior Resident posts (3-year tenure under 7th CPC) across 23 departments - 19 posts on July 8 and 41 posts on July 9, 2026.

faq.question: Q2. When and where is the ESIC Chennai walk-in interview 2026?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview is scheduled on July 8, 2026 (Wednesday) and July 9, 2026 (Thursday) at ESIC Medical College & Hospital, Ashok Pillar Road, K.K. Nagar, Chennai – 600 078. Candidates must report between 9:00 AM and 11:00 AM for document verification.

faq.question: Q3. What is the salary for ESIC Senior Resident posts under 7th CPC?
faq.answer: The basic pay is ₹67,700/- per month at Pay Level-11 under the 7th Pay Commission. The gross monthly emoluments (as of January 2026) are approximately ₹1,60,045/-, inclusive of applicable allowances.

faq.question: Q4. Who is eligible to apply for ESIC KK Nagar Senior Resident posts?
faq.answer: Candidates holding MD / MS / DNB in the relevant specialty, or M.Sc. with Ph.D. in applicable pre-clinical subjects are eligible. Age limit is 45 years for clinical specialties and 50 years for pre-clinical departments. Eligibility must conform to NMC-TEQ 2025 and PGMER 2023 standards.

faq.question: Q5. Is there any written exam for ESIC Senior Resident recruitment in Chennai?
faq.answer: Normally, selection is based on walk-in interview performance. However, if applicants exceed 10 times the number of vacancies per post, the authorities may conduct a screening MCQ test to shortlist candidates at a 1:5 ratio. Screening test marks do not affect the final selection.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for ESIC Senior Resident 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is ₹500/- for UR, OBC, and EWS candidates, payable by Demand Draft only in favour of "ESI Fund Account No. 1" payable at Chennai. SC, ST, PwD, women, and ex-servicemen are exempt from the fee.

faq.question: Q7. Can a doctor with a General Medicine degree apply for ICU or Emergency Medicine at ESIC Chennai?
faq.answer: Yes. For ICU, candidates with MD/DNB in General Medicine are eligible as Preferential Order 2. For Emergency Medicine, candidates with MD/DNB/MS in General Medicine, Anaesthesia, Respiratory Medicine, General Surgery, or Orthopaedics are eligible in addition to Emergency Medicine specialists.

faq.question: Q8. How many posts are reserved for PwD candidates in ESIC Senior Resident 2026?
faq.answer: 17 out of 60 posts are horizontally reserved for Persons with Benchmark Disability (PwBD) in identified specialties. A minimum of 40% disability, certified by a duly constituted government Medical Board, is required.

faq.question: Q9. Is private practice allowed during the ESIC Senior Resident tenure?
faq.answer: No. Private practice of any kind is strictly prohibited during the 3-year contractual engagement with ESIC.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I download the official notification and application form?
faq.answer: The official PDF notification including the application form (Annexure C) is available on the ESIC official recruitment portal.

title: ESIC Medical College & Hospital Chennai Senior Resident Recruitment 2026 – Walk in for 60 Posts
